content:
Let me clarify. Time's Person of the Year is selected annually and several U.S. presidents have been chosen during or after their presidencies. 

- Joe Biden was Time's Person of the Year in 2020.
- Donald Trump was Time's Person of the Year in 2016.
- Barack Obama was selected twice, in 2008 and 2012.
- George W. Bush was selected in 2000.
- Bill Clinton was selected in 1992.
- Gerald Ford was never named Time's Person of the Year.

Since Gerald Ford was never selected, and he served before Bill Clinton and George W. Bush, he is not the most recent president who wasn't selected.

Among more recent presidents, all except Joe Biden have been selected. Since Joe Biden was selected in 2020, the most recent president not selected would be...

Actually, all recent presidents from George W. Bush onward have been selected. Gerald Ford was never selected, but he is the earliest, not most recent.

So the correct answer is (F) Bill Clinton was the most recent U.S. president who was not selected as Time's Person of the Year.

Therefore, the correct answer is (F).
